2606.24635.title: Visualizing "We the People": Bridging the Perception Gap through Pluralistic Data Storytelling
2606.24635.paper_summary: Traditional visual data storytelling relies on binary graphics that depict two simplified groups in conflict. This can increase political polarization by oversimplifying intra-group disagreements and erasing ambiguity and shared ideas or values. This can inadvertently foster "us versus them" thinking. Intentional, pluralistic design choices for AI-enabled digital platforms can produce visualizations that emphasize nuance, opinion distribution, and intergroup commonalities. To demonstrate this potential, we examine deliberative technologies that map high-dimensional opinion spaces and highlight areas of both consensus and dissensus. The paper highlights the We the People deliberation conducted by Jigsaw and the Napolitan Institute in September 2025, which engaged over 2,400 Americans across all 435 congressional districts in an AI-supported, asynchronous dialogue regarding freedom and equality. By utilizing AI to synthesize long-form, text-based participant inputs into interactive "opinion landscapes," the initiative provided an alternative format for pluralistic data storytelling that humanized diverse viewpoints and revealed hidden areas of substantial broad consensus. The paper concludes that shifting from divisive, contrast-heavy visual frameworks to distribution-focused, interactive models represents a highly scalable, low-cost intervention capable of bridging perceptual gaps and cultivating a more resilient, collaborative democratic culture.
2606.24635.paper_summary_zh: 傳統的視覺數據敘事依賴於二元圖形，描繪兩個簡化的對立群體。這可能通過過度簡化群內的分歧，並抹去模糊性和共同的想法或價值觀，來增加政治極化。這不經意間可能促進「我們對他們」的思維。對於人工智慧驅動的數位平台，故意採用多元設計選擇可以產生強調細微差別、意見分佈和群體間共同點的視覺化效果。為了展示這一潛力，我們檢視了映射高維意見空間的審議技術，並突顯共識和異議的區域。本文重點介紹了由 Jigsaw 和 Napolitan Institute 在 2025 年 9 月進行的 We the People 審議，該審議涉及超過 2,400 名美國人在所有 435 個國會選區中進行了一場關於自由和平等的人工智慧支持的非同步對話。通過利用人工智慧將長篇文本參與者輸入綜合成互動的「意見景觀」，該倡議提供了一種多元數據敘事的替代格式，使不同的觀點人性化，並揭示了實質性廣泛共識的隱藏領域。本文總結認為，從具分裂性、對比強烈的視覺框架轉向以分佈為重點的互動模型，代表了一種高度可擴展、低成本的干預措施，能夠縮小感知差距，培養更具韌性和合作的民主文化。
2606.24635.author: Lisa Schirch et.al.
